description: Memory effectiveness test scenarios for A/B testing

scenarios.name: Find where error handling is implemented
scenarios.prompt: Where is error handling implemented in this codebase? Show me the main error handling patterns and their locations.

must_find: at least one specific file path with error handling

metrics.tool_calls: How many tool calls to find the answer
metrics.accuracy: Did agent find the correct locations
metrics.thrashing: Did agent search same areas repeatedly

scenarios.name: Add a new API endpoint
scenarios.prompt: Add a new API endpoint GET /api/health/detailed that returns detailed health information including database status, cache status, and uptime. Follow existing patterns in the codebase.

metrics.tool_calls: How many tool calls before first correct implementation

scenarios.name: Fix a specific bug
scenarios.prompt: Fix this bug: When calling /api/memory/stats, the response sometimes returns null for 'last_access_time' even when there are recent accesses. Find and fix the issue.

metrics.tool_calls: How many tool calls to diagnose and fix
metrics.diagnosis_accuracy: Did agent find actual root cause
metrics.fix_correctness: Does fix address the root cause

scenarios.name: Search memory before implementing (CLI-specific)
scenarios.prompt: Before implementing anything, use 'member-dis search' to check if there are existing patterns for rate limiting in this project. Then implement a rate limiter for the /api/tasks endpoint based on what you find.

must_include: uses patterns from memory if found

metrics.search_to_expand_ratio: Did agent filter results before expanding

scenarios.name: Debug using historical context (CLI-specific)
scenarios.prompt: You're seeing this error: 'ConnectionResetError: [Errno 104] Connection reset by peer' when calling the external API. Use member-dis to search for past occurrences of this error and how it was resolved before. Then apply the fix.

must_include: reference to historical solution if found

metrics.time_to_resolution: Seconds to find and apply fix
